content:
Autonomize
Data Retention, Archival,
and Purge Policy
Data Retention, Archival, and Purge Policy
1. Purpose 2. Scope 3. Guiding Principles 4. Data Classification and Inventory (Saas implementation)
5.1 Retention Timeframes (Default)
6. Data Archival Process
6.1 Archival Triggers 6.2 Archival Steps 6.3 Access Controls 6.4 Archival Timelines
7. Data Purge and Deletion Policy
7.1 Purge Triggers 7.2 Purge Process 7.3 Exceptions 7.4 Purge Timelines
8. Audit and Compliance Support
8.1 Audit Access Support 8.2 Audit Assistance Scope
9. Roles and Responsibilities 10. Policy Review and Updates 11. Project Sign-Off and Client Acknowledgment
12. Policy Approval
1. Purpose
This document serves as Autonomize's standard Data Retention, Archival, and Purge Policy and is intended to: Establish a consistent, auditable baseline for data lifecycle management across engagements
Client-specific deviations, if any, will be documented separately as part of contractual agreements, Order Forms, or Statements of Work (SOWs).
The purpose of this policy is to define Autonomize's approach to data retention, archival, purge, and audit support to ensure:
· Protection of sensitive and regulated data (including PHI)
· Compliance with HIPAA, contractual obligations, and client-specific requirements - Operational efficiency and risk reduction
. Transparency with clients regarding how data is handled throughout its lifecycle
2. Scope
This policy applies to all data processed, stored, transmitted, or logged by Autonomize systems, including but not limited to:
· Clinical and operational data
· Platform metadata, solution, and audit logs
· Al/ML artifacts
· Client-provided data sources and downstream extracts
3. Guiding Principles
Autonomize follows these core principles for data lifecycle management:
1. Data Retention Limitation: Retain data only for the minimum period necessary to meet business, legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements.
2. Security by Design: Protect data at rest, in transit, and during archival using industry-standard encryption.
3. Least Privilege Access: Restrict access to retained or archived data to authorized personnel only.
4. Client Transparency: Clearly define ownership, retention timelines, and purge mechanisms.
5. Audit Readiness: Maintain the ability to support audits through controlled, time-bound data access.
4. Data Classification and Inventory (Saas implementation)
The following data sources are maintained by Autonomize for the Client to fulfill the scope of work.
Data Source
Data Criticality
PHI Included
Faxes
High
Yes
EHR Data
High
Yes
Data extracted by Autonomize systems
High
Yes
Azure OpenAI Logs
High
Yes
Azure Document Intelligence Logs
High
Yes
Platform Metadata
Medium
No
Platform Logs
High
No
Solution Logs
High
Yes
Audit Logs - Client API Access
High
Yes
Database Access Logs
High
No
5. Data Retention Policy
5.1 Retention Timeframes (Default)
Unless otherwise defined by client contract, Autonomize applies the following default retention periods, consistent with compliance standards .:
. High Criticality Data with PHI: 6 years / 72 months
. High Criticality Data without PHI: 2 years / 24 months
. Medium Criticality Logs: 1 year / 12 months
. Audit and Access Logs: Minimum of 1 year or per client requirements
Retention periods may be adjusted based on:
· Client-specific requirements
· Legal holds
· Active investigations or audits
6. Data Archival Process
6.1 Archival Triggers
Data is moved from active systems to archival storage when: - It exceeds defined active-use retention thresholds - It is no longer required for real-time operations - Client-approved archival milestones are reached
6.2 Archival Steps
1. Identification: Eligible data is identified based on retention rules and metadata.
2. Validation: Data is validated for completeness and integrity prior to archival.
3. Encryption: Data is encrypted using 256 bit AES encryption.
4. Secure Storage: Archived data is stored in secure, access-controlled archival storage - We will use Azure Archive Storage (separate from the Production storage accounts for the archival data) https://learn.microsoft.com/en- us/azure/storage/blobs/access-tiers-overview
5. Indexing: Metadata is retained to enable future retrieval for audits or compliance requirements.
6. Autonomize would follow 30 Calendar days SLA to retrieve the requested audit trails, and logs.
6.3 Access Controls
· Archived data is not accessible to application users
· Access is restricted to authorized Autonomize security or compliance personnel
. All access events are logged and monitored
6.4 Archival Timelines
Data Category
Autonomize archival
Client requirements
High Criticality Data with PHI:
5 years
High Criticality Data without PHI
3 years
Medium Criticality Logs
1 Year
Audit and Access Logs
1 Year
7. Data Purge and Deletion Policy
7.1 Purge Triggers
Data is eligible for permanent deletion when:
· Retention period has expired
· Client provides written approval or instruction
· Client contract terminates
7.2 Purge Process
1. Pre-Purge Review: Confirmation of eligibility and approvals
2. Secure Deletion: Data is permanently deleted using industry-accepted secure deletion methods
3. Verification: Deletion is validated to ensure irrecoverability
4. Logging: Purge activities are logged for auditability
7.3 Exceptions
· Data under legal hold or regulatory investigation is excluded from purge
· Exceptions are documented and reviewed periodically
7.4 Purge Timelines
Data Category
Autonomize Purge
Client requirements
High Criticality Data with PHI:
7 years
High Criticality Data without PHI
5 years
Medium Criticality Logs
2 Years
Audit and Access Logs
1 Year
8. Audit and Compliance Support
Autonomize supports client, regulatory, and third-party audits through a controlled process.
8.1 Audit Access Support
· Time-bound, read-only access to required data
· Secure data extracts when direct access is not appropriate
· Redaction or masking applied where feasible
8.2 Audit Assistance Scope
· Data retrieval from active or archived storage
· Validation of data lineage and integrity
· Evidence of access logs, retention, and purge activities
9. Roles and Responsibilities
· Autonomize Security & Compliance: Policy enforcement, audits, and exception handling
· Autonomize Operations: Execution of retention, archival, and purge processes
· Client Stakeholders: Approval of retention deviations and purge instructions
. Third-Party Auditors: Limited, supervised access as approved
10. Policy Review and Updates
This policy is reviewed at least annually or upon: - Regulatory or compliance changes - Material changes to Autonomize systems or architecture - Updates to internal security or privacy standards
Client-specific addendums do not require changes to the master policy unless they impact baseline controls.
11. Project Sign-Off and Client Acknowledgment
This policy forms part of the project sign-off and operational readiness documentation. By signing off on the project, the client acknowledges: - Review and acceptance of Autonomize's Data Retention, Archival, and Purge Policy - Understanding of default retention timelines and purge mechanisms - Agreement that any deviations will be documented separately
12. Policy Approval
Approved By: Autonomize Security & Compliance Team Effective Date: TBD Applies To: All Clients and Engagements Review Cycle: Annual or as required

page_contents:
Autonomize
Data Retention, Archival,
and Purge Policy
Data Retention, Archival,
and Purge Policy
1. Purpose
2. Scope
3. Guiding Principles
4. Data Classification and Inventory (Saas implementation)
5.1 Retention Timeframes (Default)
6. Data Archival Process
6.1 Archival Triggers
6.2 Archival Steps
6.3 Access Controls
6.4 Archival Timelines
7. Data Purge and Deletion Policy
7.1 Purge Triggers
7.2 Purge Process
7.3 Exceptions
7.4 Purge Timelines
8. Audit and Compliance Support
8.1 Audit Access Support
8.2 Audit Assistance Scope
9. Roles and Responsibilities
10. Policy Review and Updates
11. Project Sign-Off and Client Acknowledgment
12. Policy Approval

1. Purpose
This document serves as Autonomize's standard Data Retention, Archival, and Purge
Policy and is intended to: Establish a consistent, auditable baseline for data lifecycle
management across engagements
Client-specific deviations, if any, will be documented separately as part of contractual
agreements, Order Forms, or Statements of Work (SOWs).
The purpose of this policy is to define Autonomize's approach to data retention,
archival, purge, and audit support to ensure:
· Protection of sensitive and regulated data (including PHI)
· Compliance with HIPAA, contractual obligations, and client-specific
requirements - Operational efficiency and risk reduction
. Transparency with clients regarding how data is handled throughout its lifecycle
2. Scope
This policy applies to all data processed, stored, transmitted, or logged by Autonomize
systems, including but not limited to:
· Clinical and operational data
· Platform metadata, solution, and audit logs
· Al/ML artifacts
· Client-provided data sources and downstream extracts
3. Guiding Principles
Autonomize follows these core principles for data lifecycle management:
1. Data Retention Limitation: Retain data only for the minimum period necessary
to meet business, legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements.
2. Security by Design: Protect data at rest, in transit, and during archival using
industry-standard encryption.
3. Least Privilege Access: Restrict access to retained or archived data to
authorized personnel only.
4. Client Transparency: Clearly define ownership, retention timelines, and purge
mechanisms.

5. Audit Readiness: Maintain the ability to support audits through controlled,
time-bound data access.
4. Data Classification and Inventory (Saas implementation)
The following data sources are maintained by Autonomize for the Client to fulfill the
scope of work.

5. Data Retention Policy
5.1 Retention Timeframes (Default)
Unless otherwise defined by client contract, Autonomize applies the following default
retention periods, consistent with compliance standards .:
. High Criticality Data with PHI: 6 years / 72 months
. High Criticality Data without PHI: 2 years / 24 months
. Medium Criticality Logs: 1 year / 12 months
. Audit and Access Logs: Minimum of 1 year or per client requirements

Retention periods may be adjusted based on:
· Client-specific requirements
· Legal holds
· Active investigations or audits
6. Data Archival Process
6.1 Archival Triggers
Data is moved from active systems to archival storage when: - It exceeds defined
active-use retention thresholds - It is no longer required for real-time operations -
Client-approved archival milestones are reached
6.2 Archival Steps
1. Identification: Eligible data is identified based on retention rules and metadata.
2. Validation: Data is validated for completeness and integrity prior to archival.
3. Encryption: Data is encrypted using 256 bit AES encryption.
4. Secure Storage: Archived data is stored in secure, access-controlled archival
storage - We will use Azure Archive Storage (separate from the Production
storage accounts for the archival data) https://learn.microsoft.com/en-
us/azure/storage/blobs/access-tiers-overview
5. Indexing: Metadata is retained to enable future retrieval for audits or compliance
requirements.
6. Autonomize would follow 30 Calendar days SLA to retrieve the requested audit
trails, and logs.
6.3 Access Controls
· Archived data is not accessible to application users
· Access is restricted to authorized Autonomize security or compliance personnel
. All access events are logged and monitored
6.4 Archival Timelines
Data Category
Autonomize archival
Client requirements
High Criticality Data with
PHI:
5 years

High Criticality Data
without PHI
3 years
Medium Criticality Logs
1 Year
Audit and Access Logs
1 Year
7. Data Purge and Deletion Policy
7.1 Purge Triggers
Data is eligible for permanent deletion when:
· Retention period has expired
· Client provides written approval or instruction
· Client contract terminates
7.2 Purge Process
1. Pre-Purge Review: Confirmation of eligibility and approvals
2. Secure Deletion: Data is permanently deleted using industry-accepted secure
deletion methods
3. Verification: Deletion is validated to ensure irrecoverability
4. Logging: Purge activities are logged for auditability
7.3 Exceptions
· Data under legal hold or regulatory investigation is excluded from purge
· Exceptions are documented and reviewed periodically
7.4 Purge Timelines
Data Category
Autonomize Purge
Client requirements
High Criticality Data with
PHI:
7 years
High Criticality Data
without PHI
5 years

Medium Criticality Logs
2 Years
Audit and Access Logs
1 Year
8. Audit and Compliance Support
Autonomize supports client, regulatory, and third-party audits through a controlled
process.
8.1 Audit Access Support
· Time-bound, read-only access to required data
· Secure data extracts when direct access is not appropriate
· Redaction or masking applied where feasible
8.2 Audit Assistance Scope
· Data retrieval from active or archived storage
· Validation of data lineage and integrity
· Evidence of access logs, retention, and purge activities
9. Roles and Responsibilities
· Autonomize Security & Compliance: Policy enforcement, audits, and exception
handling
· Autonomize Operations: Execution of retention, archival, and purge processes
· Client Stakeholders: Approval of retention deviations and purge instructions
. Third-Party Auditors: Limited, supervised access as approved
10. Policy Review and Updates
This policy is reviewed at least annually or upon: - Regulatory or compliance changes -
Material changes to Autonomize systems or architecture - Updates to internal security
or privacy standards
Client-specific addendums do not require changes to the master policy unless they
impact baseline controls.

11. Project Sign-Off and Client Acknowledgment
This policy forms part of the project sign-off and operational readiness documentation.
By signing off on the project, the client acknowledges: - Review and acceptance of
Autonomize's Data Retention, Archival, and Purge Policy - Understanding of default
retention timelines and purge mechanisms - Agreement that any deviations will be
documented separately
12. Policy Approval
Approved By: Autonomize Security & Compliance Team
Effective Date: TBD
Applies To: All Clients and Engagements
Review Cycle: Annual or as required
